title: Lights, Camara,Blocks?
description: Lights, Camera, Blocks! turns Minecraft into a creative photo and film studio. Capture, edit, and showcase stunning moments with Exposure and visual mods — all optimized for smooth performance and cinematic visuals.
body:
Lights, Camera, Blocks! transforms Minecraft into your very own cinematic studio. Whether you're a creator, photographer, or builder, this modpack gives you the perfect tools to capture breathtaking moments, build film sets, and craft visual masterpieces — all while keeping gameplay smooth and stable.

🌅 Photography & Cinematics

Exposure, Exposure: Expanded, Exposure: Polaroid, and Exposure Catalog bring full camera functionality — lenses, filters, lighting tools, and even film development.

Icon Exporter lets you easily capture and export clean item or block renders.

Knowlogy Book and Exposure Knowlogy provide in-game documentation for mastering your tools.

⚙️ Performance & Optimization

Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, and Entity Culling keep everything running smoothly for lag-free recording and rendering.

Fabric API, Architectury API, Forge Config API Port, Cyclops Core, and owo-lib ensure top-notch mod compatibility.

🎨 Visuals & Presentation

Iris Shaders for stunning lighting and cinematic depth.

Not Enough Animations for smoother player movement in videos.

Lavender adds a calm, immersive atmosphere perfect for creative sessions.

AppleSkin and Xaero’s Maps provide helpful overlays for creators and explorers alike.

🧭 Utility & Ease of Use

Mod Menu for quick configuration and control.

Text Placeholder API supports advanced customization and camera naming.

Xaero’s World Map & Minimap help you find ideal shot locations easily.

💡 Perfect For:

🎥 Minecraft filmmakers and machinima creators
📸 Screenshot artists and builders
🎞️ Content creators who want cinematic flair without performance drops

✨ In Short

Lights, Camera, Blocks! is where creativity meets performance — giving you every tool to make Minecraft not just a game, but your canvas for storytelling, art, and film.
